implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0

completion_promises.criterion_text: Every state-machine transition listed above triggers exactly one publish call
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented PipelineLifecycleEmitter with eight emit_* methods (one per lifecycle event) plus an on_transition dispatcher driven by TRANSITION_TO_EMITTER. TestEmitFiresExactlyOnce asserts each emit_* method invokes its mocked publisher method exactly once and TestOnTransitionRouting asserts each declared (from_state, to_state) pair routes to the correct publisher method. Unknown transitions are a no-op (test_unknown_transition_is_noop).

completion_promises.criterion_text: correlation_id threaded from BuildQueuedPayload through every downstream event (Group D @edge-case scenario)
completion_promises.evidence: BuildContext carries the originating correlation_id; every emit method either populates the field directly (v2.2 payloads with correlation_id as a declared field) or attaches it post-construction via attach_correlation_id (v1 payloads with extra='ignore'). attach_correlation_id uses object.__setattr__ to bypass pydantic's field validation so the publisher's getattr(payload, 'correlation_id', None) threads it onto the envelope without modifying nats-core schemas. TestPayloadShape verifies all eight payload types carry the correlation_id; TestScenarioGroupD::test_full_lifecycle_threads_single_correlation_id runs the complete lifecycle and asserts every published payload shares the same correlation_id.

emit_paused_then_interrupt awaits emit_paused first, then awaits the injected interrupt callable. TestPausedBeforeInterrupt::test_publish_runs_strictly_before_interrupt records the call order in a list and asserts ['publish', 'interrupt']. test_interrupt_still_runs_even_if_publish_fails asserts that even when the publish raises PublishFailure, the interrupt still fires (the SQLite PAUSED row has already been written by the state machine, so consumers see consistent state on replay).

completion_promises.criterion_text: A publish raising PublishFailure is logged but does NOT revert the SQLite row — state remains as written (Group E @data-integrity)
completion_promises.evidence: _safe_publish wraps every publisher invocation in try/except PublishFailure; the exception is logged at ERROR with subject + cause, and never re-raised. The SQLite row is therefore untouched (the emitter never touches SQLite — that is the state machine's responsibility — but it can never trigger a rollback by raising). TestPublishFailureSwallowed::test_emit_started_swallows_publish_failure_and_logs verifies the log message; test_every_emit_method_swallows_publish_failure parametrises across all seven other emit_* methods.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Progress publisher fires at least every PipelineConfig.progress_interval_seconds during RUNNING (uses injected Clock; test uses FakeClock to advance)
completion_promises.evidence: start_progress_loop launches an asyncio.Task that loops on `await clock.sleep(progress_interval_seconds)` then calls emit_progress with the snapshot returned by an injected get_progress callable. The Clock protocol is satisfied in production by RealClock (asyncio.sleep + time.monotonic) and in tests by FakeClock (deterministic advance with awaited Event waiters). TestProgressLoop::test_progress_fires_after_each_interval_tick advances FakeClock three times by the configured interval and asserts the publisher's await_count climbs to 3. test_progress_does_not_fire_before_interval advances by half an interval and asserts await_count remains 0. start_progress_loop and stop_progress_loop are both idempotent per build_id.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Progress publisher also fires on wave boundaries from autobuild_runner (hook point documented in code)
completion_promises.evidence: emit_wave_boundary_progress is the named hook for autobuild_runner; it delegates to emit_progress so the wire format is identical, but its existence as a separate method makes the wave-boundary emission point auditable in code. TestWaveBoundary::test_wave_boundary_calls_publish_build_progress asserts the call invokes publish_build_progress with the correct wave/percent/elapsed and that correlation_id is threaded.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests mock PipelinePublisher; assert method called with correct payload shape
completion_promises.evidence: Every test in test_pipeline_lifecycle.py constructs an AsyncMock with the eight publisher method names and injects it into PipelineLifecycleEmitter. TestPayloadShape (8 tests) inspects the call_args of each mocked publisher method and asserts the payload is the correct typed Pydantic model with the expected field values (feature_id, build_id, wave, gate_mode, decision, etc.) plus the threaded correlation_id.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Scenario test 'All lifecycle events share originating correlation identifier' passes (Group D @edge-case)
completion_promises.evidence: TestScenarioGroupD::test_full_lifecycle_threads_single_correlation_id runs a complete lifecycle (started → progress → stage_complete → paused_then_interrupt → resumed → wave-boundary progress → complete), gathers every payload sent to every mocked publisher method, and asserts that getattr(payload, 'correlation_id', None) == ctx.correlation_id for all 7 payloads. test_failed_terminal_threads_correlation and test_cancelled_terminal_threads_correlation cover the alternative terminal paths.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Code follows the .claude/rules/code-style.md guidance: snake_case functions/variables, PascalCase classes, UPPER_CASE constants, type hints throughout, max 88-char lines (Black-compatible), f-strings for messages, dataclasses + frozen + slots where appropriate, context managers via try/except. The repository does not currently wire ruff/black into pytest; project CI applies those checks. The 44 new tests pass and no warnings remain after the asyncio-mark cleanup.
